# services.lokinet

<!-- AUTO-GENERATED by scripts/nix-options.sh. Do not edit by hand. -->

All options under `services.lokinet`.

| Option | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `services.lokinet.enable` | `boolean` | Whether to enable Lokinet daemon. |
| `services.lokinet.package` | `package` | The lokinet package to use. |
| `services.lokinet.settings` | `open submodule of attribute set of section of an INI file (attrs of INI atom (null, bool, int, float or string) or a list of them for duplicate keys)` | Configuration for Lokinet. Currently, the best way to view the available settings is by generating a config file using `lokinet -g`. |
| `services.lokinet.settings.dns.bind` | `string` | Address to bind to for handling DNS requests. |
| `services.lokinet.settings.dns.upstream` | `list of string` | Upstream resolver(s) to use as fallback for non-loki addresses. Multiple values accepted. |
| `services.lokinet.settings.network.exit` | `boolean` | Whether to act as an exit node. Beware that this increases demand on the server and may pose liability concerns. Enable at your own risk. |
| `services.lokinet.settings.network.exit-node` | `null or (list of string)` | Specify a `.loki` address and an optional ip range to use as an exit broker. See <http://probably.loki/wiki/index.php?title=Exit_Nodes> for a list of exit nodes. |
| `services.lokinet.settings.network.keyfile` | `null or string` | The private key to persist address with. If not specified the address will be ephemeral. This keyfile is generated automatically if the specified file doesn't exist. |
| `services.lokinet.useLocally` | `boolean` | Whether to use Lokinet locally. |
